summary: visibility traffic-citations parses citation_snapshots.raw_json and exact-matches URL fields instead of substring-matching the whole blob.
reason: strings.Contains(string(c.RawJSON), p.URL) attributed citations to the wrong page whenever a short page URL like example.com/blog appeared as a prefix of a longer citation URL (example.com/blog/post-a) or as a substring inside an image src, canonical tag, or author link embedded in the raw JSON. The walker recurses through the parsed JSON and exact-matches normalized URLs, eliminating prefix-and-substring false positives.
